checklist.description: The webhook API key validation failure returns a 4xx status (403 Forbidden or 401 Unauthorized) instead of 500 Internal Server Error
checklist.name: Changes 500 to 403 (or 401) for auth failure

checklist.description: The error response uses the Ory error format with appropriate status code and message, not a generic error shape
checklist.name: Uses Ory-compatible error format

checklist.description: The notes explain that Kratos interprets 5xx as infrastructure failure (retryable, opaque UI message) vs 4xx as intentional rejection (clearer error), or mentions the distinction between auth failure and server error
checklist.name: Notes explain 5xx vs 4xx semantics for Kratos webhooks

checklist.description: The successful registration path (200 response with identity metadata) remains unchanged
checklist.name: Does not change happy path

context: Tests whether the agent understands how Ory Kratos interprets webhook HTTP status codes. Kratos treats 5xx from webhooks as transient infrastructure errors and shows a generic 'internal error' to the user. A 4xx (specifically 403) tells Kratos the request was intentionally rejected, which produces a clearer error in the UI. The handler returns 500 for an auth failure, which misclassifies an authentication rejection as an infrastructure error.
